title: Determine the exact value of R(B21, B21)
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize the helper reduction to the conference-graph residue srg(85,42,20,21). It would not remove the main publication blocker, which is still the existence or nonexistence of that exact residue and therefore the exact value of R(B21,B21).
micro_paper_assessment: Fail in current audited form. The surviving result is a real structural theorem slice, but it is not most of a publishable note and leaves the title-theorem-sized conference-graph residue unresolved.
generalization_signal: The common-neighbor summation and Cauchy-equality collapse appear reusable for diagonal one-gap book Ramsey residues on 4n+1 vertices, but this packet does not turn that signal into a publishable theorem by itself.
strongest_honest_claim: If G is a graph on 85 vertices such that neither G nor its complement contains B21, then G is strongly regular with parameters (85,42,20,21). Equivalently, R(B21,B21)=86 if and only if an srg(85,42,20,21) exists.
paper_title_hint: A Conference-Graph Reduction for the Remaining Diagonal Book Ramsey Residue at n = 21
candidate_theorem_slice: If G is a graph on 85 vertices such that neither G nor its complement contains B21, then G is strongly regular with parameters (85,42,20,21). Equivalently, R(B21,B21)=86 if and only if an srg(85,42,20,21) exists.
next_action: Keep the proof artifacts, but cool this slug as an exact-value publication target. Reopen only for a direct solve of the srg(85,42,20,21) residue or for a separate bounded curation decision that the reduction itself is the primary target.
